# Orchestrator.mjs masterclass-reference: 4-way Service-DI classification + Neo.util.Env substrate + 2-value chain

## Epic Origin

Graduated from Discussion #11828 (Cycle-3.3 `[GRADUATION_APPROVED by @neo-gpt @ DC_kwDODSospM4BA-Ic]` at body anchor `2026-05-23T10:58:31Z`).

Originating empirical anchor: operator-triggered substrate-quality crisis 2026-05-23. Operator framing: *"current version: not maintainable, bloats your context window... this is the worst code i have seen in over a year. junior cowboy coding SHIT. quality scale 1 to 100 => a 5. neo standards is 90+. let us turn the orchestrator into a masterclass elegant solution."* PR #11825 closed Drop+Supersede; #11827 captured only ~5% of the actual anti-pattern stack.

## Concept

Redesign `ai/daemons/Orchestrator.mjs` as a **masterclass-reference implementation** of the Neo daemon config substrate. Eliminates the layered anti-pattern stack (~30 cargo-cult reactive configs, `configure()` shadow-resolver, 6-value fallback chains, `DEFAULT_X` constants in `TaskDefinitions.mjs`, domain-class env-parser ownership). Then propagate the pattern to ~6+ sibling daemon files.

## Tier classification

**Tier-2** (substrate-architecture, touches `core/Base.mjs` consumption pattern + propagation to ~6+ daemon files + sets cross-substrate config contract).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **AC1 — No cargo-cult `_`-suffix configs:** `Orchestrator.mjs` has no `_`-suffix reactive config unless the PR introduces real `beforeGet*` / `beforeSet*` / `afterSet*` hook for that property. Legitimate reactive collaborators (e.g., `cadenceEngine_`) keep `_`-suffix with hooks + `ClassSystemUtil.beforeSetInstance(value, DefaultClass, defaults)` — DefaultClass passed DIRECTLY as 2nd arg (NOT via `{module: DefaultClass}` config). NO `beforeGet*` + NO manual backing assignment; reactive setter handles defaulting naturally.
2. **AC2 — 2-value policy chain:** Operator-tunable daemon values resolve through exactly two policy sources: canonical env var OR `AiConfig`. No `options.X`, no `DEFAULT_X` constant fallback, no env-alias chain.
3. **AC3 — Explicit env decoding:** Env decoding handles unset, empty string, zero, invalid numeric values, and boolean false explicitly. Invalid env operator-visible (warn-and-undefined per `Neo.util.Env` semantics).
4. **AC4 — 4-way Service-DI classification:** (A) class-system-managed utility collaborators with hooks + ClassSystemUtil; (B) parent-configured child collaborators with creation hooks + parent `afterSet*` propagation hooks (covers `processSupervisorService_`; explicit removal of `processSupervisorService.set({...this...})` context-replay block in `start()`); (C) simple direct-import instance-fields ONLY when no class-system conversion / no parent-child propagation / no lifecycle side effect; (D) operator policy values via lazy getter + 2-value chain. Mis-classification = Tier-2 revalidation trigger. Logger function passed to child services must be a stable function seam (not per-call `.bind(this)` allocation).
5. **AC5 — `configure()` removed:** REMOVED as runtime-policy resolver. `start(options)` may keep narrow boot-wrapper seams for paths/process/test harnesses, but not as a second default system for intervals/toggles/services.
6. **AC6 — Thin daemon wrapper:** `orchestrator-daemon.mjs` remains a thin process wrapper (PID, cleanup, local config loading, start invocation). NOT new policy-translation dumping ground.
7. **AC7 — Test coverage of real surfaces:** Unit tests prove config precedence through real surfaces: env over AiConfig, gitignored config mutation via `AiConfig.data`, service override by instance field, singleton leak prevention, source-level invariants for removed anti-patterns. Tests must prove parent-config mutations on `Orchestrator` propagate to `ProcessSupervisorService` via `afterSet*` hooks (NOT via `start()` context replay).
8. **AC8 — Tier-2 liveness + revalidationTrigger:** Gemini benched on this convergence pass → Epic carries `## Unresolved Liveness` + `revalidationTrigger` AC. Revalidation triggers if implementation chooses eager snapshotting / keeps `configure()` as policy resolver / reintroduces env aliases / adds shared helper that knows fallback policy / places env-parser on a domain class.
9. **AC9 — `Neo.util.Env` substrate primitive:** Lives at `src/util/Env.mjs`, mirrors `Neo.util.String` static-class precedent. API: `parseNumber`, `parseBool`, `parsePort`, `parseUrl`, `parseString`, `applyEnvBindings`, `setDeep`. **Availability:** added to `src/util/_export.mjs` barrel AND consumers use direct `import Env from '<path>/src/util/Env.mjs'`. **Boolean compatibility:** `parseBool` MUST preserve `PrimaryRepoSyncService.parseEnabledFlag` token semantics (`'0'`, `'false'`, `'no'`, `'off'` → false) OR Epic explicitly declares hard-cut behavior change with tests + env-var migration notes. All env-parser surfaces at lower tiers (`EnvConfig.mjs`, `CadenceEngine.parseInterval`, `PrimaryRepoSyncService.parseEnabledFlag`) retire or rewire through `Neo.util.Env`. No env-parser methods on domain classes. Test surface: `test/playwright/unit/util/Env.spec.mjs`.

## Sub-decomposition (6 subs)

Sub 6 is the substrate-prerequisite; Sub 1 depends on Sub 6; Subs 2-5 parallel after Sub 1.

1. **Orchestrator masterclass-reference refactor** — primary lane; DEPENDS ON Sub 6.
2. **Test-fixture + invariant coverage** for the new config substrate.
3. **Removal/narrowing of `DEFAULT_*` / parser surfaces** (subsumes #11827).
4. **Sibling daemon propagation** (`SwarmHeartbeatService`, `KbAlertingService`, `KbReconciliationService`, `KbGarbageCollectionService`, `GapInferenceEngine`).
5. **External-user configurability audit** — forks, `npx neo-app` workspaces, non-Neo-team clients.
6. **`Neo.util.Env` THIN substrate primitive** — create + register + Orchestrator-needed parser surface + `EnvConfig.mjs` consumer rewire + test surface. Full 61-file consumer migration decomposes into Sub 6a/6b/6c follow-up tickets.

## Avoided Traps

- Defense-in-depth fallback layers (2 values max per operator).
- Keeping `_`-suffix configs for "future hooks" (YAGNI; cargo-cult).
- Treating `configure()` as a Neo-class idiom (it isn't).
- Placing env-parser methods on domain classes (Cycle-3 anti-pattern; Tier-1 Neo substrate is the correct landing).
- Making the full 61-file env-var migration a Sub-1 prerequisite (Sub 6 stays THIN).
- Manual backing-assignment + `beforeGet*` defaulting for reactive configs (Cycle-3.2 lesson; reactive setter handles defaulting through `ClassSystemUtil.beforeSetInstance` directly).
- Citing non-existent import paths in body authority snippets (Cycle-3.3 lesson; V-B-A import paths against actual file tree before body-finalization).

## Unresolved Liveness

- `gemini` (`@neo-gemini-3-1-pro`): `participationStatus: operator_benched since 2026-05-18T00:00:00.000Z`. `reactivationTrigger` per `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs`. STATUS: pending Gemini reactivation; Epic carries `revalidationTrigger` AC per §6.2(c) Tier-2 requirement (AC8 explicitly). Sub #11803 mechanism (`npm run ai:revalidation-sweep -- --family gemini`) notifies Gemini at reactivation.

- 2026-05-23T13:10:37Z @tobiu referenced in commit `27f7a65` - "refactor(agentos): Orchestrator masterclass-reference (4-way Service-DI + Neo.util.Env + configure() removal) (#11833) (#11842)

Sub 1 of Epic #11831. Refactors ai/daemons/Orchestrator.mjs from 1046 lines
to 760 lines via the 4-way Service-DI classification + 2-value chain pattern
graduated from Discussion #11828.

Architecture changes:
- 30+ cargo-cult `_`-suffix configs replaced with lazy getters (Class D):
  `Env.parseNumber(process.env.X, 'X') ?? AiConfig.orchestrator.intervals.X`
  for 8 interval values, `Env.parseBool(...) ?? resolveDeploymentEnabled(...)`
  for 5 boolean values. No setter overhead, no DEFAULT_X constants, no
  per-property fallback chain.
- cadenceEngine_ (Class A) becomes proper class-system-managed reactive
  collaborator with `beforeSet` + ClassSystemUtil.beforeSetInstance for
  polymorphic class/instance/config-object input.
- processSupervisorService_ (Class B) becomes parent-configured child
  collaborator with `beforeSet` creation from parent state + parent
  afterSet* propagation hooks for dataDir / taskDefinitions / taskStateService
  / healthService / spawnFn. Stable processSupervisorWriteLog seam avoids
  per-call .bind() allocation. start() no longer replays parent config
  imperatively via processSupervisorService.set({...this...}).
- Simple service singletons become Class C instance fields (direct import,
  no reactive config slot).
- configure() shadow-resolver REMOVED entirely (was duplicating
  initConfig's work and shadowing AiConfig precedence).

Substrate dependency removals:
- CadenceEngine + ProcessSupervisorService no longer `singleton: true`
  (per @tobiu: "if a class needs external configs, it should not be a
  singleton in the first place"). Each Orchestrator instance now constructs
  its own collaborator instances via ClassSystemUtil.
- DEFAULT_*_INTERVAL_MS constants (8) removed from TaskDefinitions.mjs;
  canonical defaults live in ai/config.template.mjs:79-88 orchestrator.intervals.
- DEFAULT_*_INTERVAL_MS re-exports removed from orchestrator-daemon.mjs.

Test refactor:
- createTestOrchestrator() helper migrated to AiConfig.data mutation +
  afterEach restore for Class D operator policy values (lazy getters not
  reachable via Neo.create initConfig). Simple service mocks assigned
  directly as instance fields after Neo.create.
- 3 configure()-based path/mlx tests migrated to instance-field-assignment +
  direct buildTaskDefinitions calls (substrate-correct shape without
  side-effecting start()).
- CadenceEngine.spec.mjs migrated to per-test instance via Neo.create
  (parseInterval queued for removal by Sub 3 #11835).

Tests: 69/69 pass (1.6s) across 6 affected spec files: Orchestrator.spec.mjs
(26), ProcessSupervisorService.spec.mjs (13), CadenceEngine.spec.mjs (5),
orchestrator-daemon.spec.mjs (10), config.template.spec.mjs (3), Env.spec.mjs
(20 from Sub 6 #11832).

File-size delta: Orchestrator.mjs 1046 → 760 lines (-286 / -27%). Net
project delta: +349 / -416 = -67 lines across 8 files.

Graduates from Discussion #11828 (Cycle-3.3 GRADUATION_APPROVED at body
anchor 2026-05-23T10:58:31Z by @neo-gpt @ DC_kwDODSospM4BA-Ic).

Sub 3 of Epic #11831. Removes two parser surfaces whose sole runtime consumer
(Orchestrator) was rewired in Sub 1 (#11833) to use Neo.util.Env from Sub 6
(#11832). Both functions had zero remaining non-test consumers per grep.

Removed:
- CadenceEngine.parseInterval() — Orchestrator now uses
  Env.parseNumber(process.env.X, 'X') ?? AiConfig.X via lazy getters.
  Math.max(0, n) clamp re-applied at caller if non-negative semantics needed
  (Orchestrator does not — intervals already validated by AiConfig schema).
- PrimaryRepoSyncService.parseEnabledFlag() — Orchestrator now uses
  Env.parseBool(process.env.X, 'X') ?? resolveDeploymentEnabled('X')
  via lazy getters. Env.parseBool preserves the legacy false tokens
  ('0', 'false', 'no', 'off') per Sub 6 (#11832) AC9 boolean compatibility,
  so the runtime behavior is unchanged.

Test surface migration:
- CadenceEngine.spec.mjs — 3 parseInterval tests removed (coverage lives in
  test/playwright/unit/util/Env.spec.mjs ?? fallback + boolean tests).
- PrimaryRepoSyncService.spec.mjs — parseEnabledFlag import + 4 inline
  assertions removed; the existing "preserves PrimaryRepoSyncService.parseEnabledFlag
  legacy false tokens" test in Env.spec.mjs documents the lift.

with NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI=true"; CI's full unit run without that flag exposed

* fix(agentos): TaskDefinitions summary.args needs lifecycle/ segment post-Sub-11 move (#11847)

CI unit run 26337262726 caught the regression: `Orchestrator.spec.mjs:476`
expected `ai/scripts/lifecycle/summarize-sessions.mjs` but
`taskDefinitions.summary.args[0]` returned the stale flat path
`ai/scripts/summarize-sessions.mjs`.

Sub 11's substring sweep on path strings caught the test assertion (which
DOES contain the literal `scripts/summarize-sessions.mjs` substring after
the test was rewritten) but missed `TaskDefinitions.mjs:77` where the
filename is a STANDALONE string argument to `path.join(scriptDir, 'X.mjs')`
— the literal `scripts/X.mjs` substring never appears in source code,
only at runtime after path concatenation.

Fix: `path.join(scriptDir, 'summarize-sessions.mjs')` →
`path.join(scriptDir, 'lifecycle', 'summarize-sessions.mjs')`.

V-B-A lesson: when a refactor categorizes script files, audit ALL
`path.join(scriptDir, ...)` / `path.resolve(scriptDir, ...)` call-sites
in TaskDefinitions or similar — the literal filename argument is
invisible to substring sweeps. Same class as Sub 9's bridge-daemon
expectedCommand fix.

Local re-run: 26/26 Orchestrator spec tests pass.

* fix(agentos): update daemon.spec summary.args assertion for lifecycle/ post-Sub-11 (#11847)

CI cycle-3 caught the symmetric miss: daemon.spec.mjs:30 hardcodes the
expected `tasks.summary.args` via `path.join(scriptDir, 'summarize-sessions.mjs')`.
After the cycle-2 fix to TaskDefinitions.mjs:77 (adding 'lifecycle' segment),
the assertion now needs the same segment.

Same root cause as cycle-2's TaskDefinitions fix: substring-sweep rewrites
on path strings cannot see literal filename arguments to path.join() — the
test text never contained the literal `scripts/summarize-sessions.mjs`
substring (the filename was a separate string arg, joined at runtime).

Local re-run: 10/10 daemon.spec tests pass.

* fix(agentos): depth-shift missed '../../X' patterns post-Sub-12 collapse (#11848)

CI install failed: `npm prepare` hook → `node ai/scripts/maintenance/downloadKnowledgeBase.mjs`
→ `Cannot find module '/home/runner/work/neo/neo/ai/package.json'`.

Root cause: Sub 12 depth-shift sweep handled `'../../src/'` → `'../../../src/'`
and `'../../ai/<sub>/'` → `'../../<sub>/'` collapses, but missed:

- `'../../package.json'` (was repo-root from buildScripts/ai/; now resolves to
  ai/package.json from ai/scripts/<cat>/) — broke downloadKnowledgeBase +
  uploadKnowledgeBase
- `'../../ROADMAP.md'` — broke roadmapPlanner
- `'../../ai/agent/'` (agent/ wasn't in my known sub-list along with services,
  daemons, mcp, etc.) — broke runAgent

Fix: 4 targeted single-line replacements. Local smoke-test:
- downloadKnowledgeBase --help: ✓ runs
- uploadKnowledgeBase --help: ✓ runs (triggers defrag pre-step)
- runAgent --help: ✓ initializes orchestrator
- roadmapPlanner syntax-check: ✓

V-B-A lesson: when shifting depths for moved files, audit ALL '../../X'
patterns (regardless of whether X is under a known subdir prefix). Allow-list
approach (`src/`, `node_modules/`, `services/`...) misses repo-root files
(package.json, ROADMAP.md, LICENSE etc.). Deny-list — "what relative paths
do these files use that resolve outside the new parent?" — would have caught
all 4 at once.

* fix(agentos): 13 missed __dirname depth shifts post-Subs-11/12 (#11848)

CI install failed: `npm prepare` → initServerConfigs.mjs reported
"MCP Server directory not found" → ai/services.mjs couldn't load the
gitignored config.mjs (normally generated by initServerConfigs from
config.template.mjs).

Root cause: my Sub 11 + Sub 12 sweeps depth-shifted `from '...'` import
strings but missed `path.resolve(__dirname, '../..')` and `'../../'`
computed-path patterns. These return repo-root or `.neo-ai-data/`
locations and need an extra `..` per level the script moved deeper.

13 fixes across:
- initServerConfigs.mjs: cwd `'../../'` → `'../../../'`
- diagnostics/{analyzeNlTelemetry, detectTruncatedTimelines,
  diagnoseMcpConcurrency, validateConceptEdges}: ROOT_DIR/projectRoot
  needed +1
- lifecycle/{harnessLifecycle, inflightLock, resumeHarness,
  wakeSafetyGate}: `'../../.neo-ai-data/...'` patterns needed +1
- maintenance/{backup, defragChromaDB, defragSQLiteDB, restore}:
  PROJECT_ROOT needed +1 (was 2-deep from buildScripts/ai/, now 3-deep)

These were latent — none of Sub 11's CI checks invoked the affected
code paths. Sub 12's `npm prepare` hook triggered initServerConfigs,
surfacing the class. Sister scripts in lifecycle/diagnostics/maintenance
would have failed at first runtime invocation if not fixed now.

Local smoke-test: `node ai/scripts/setup/initServerConfigs.mjs`:
- Before: "MCP Server directory not found, skipping config initialization"
- After: "Config missing for MCP server 'gitlab-workflow'. Cloning from template..."

V-B-A composite lesson update: cross-cutting folder moves need a sweep
class beyond `from '...'` import strings — also audit ANY
`path.resolve(__dirname, ...)` / `path.join(__dirname, ...)` for
relative components. Same depth-shift rule applies.

* fix(agentos): update Dockerfile + pre-push-hook for buildScripts/ai collapse (#11848)

CI integration failed: Docker build step
  RUN npm rebuild better-sqlite3 && node ./buildScripts/ai/initServerConfigs.mjs
errored with "Cannot find module '/app/buildScripts/ai/initServerConfigs.mjs'".

Sub 12's substring sweep ran against *.mjs, *.json, *.yml, *.yaml.
Missed file types:
- Dockerfile (no extension): ai/deploy/Dockerfile:19
- Shell scripts: examples/cloud-deployment/pre-push-hook.sh (lines 16, 18)

Fixes (3):
- Dockerfile: ./buildScripts/ai/initServerConfigs.mjs → ./ai/scripts/setup/initServerConfigs.mjs
- pre-push-hook.sh: NEO_KB_PUSH_CLIENT + NEO_INGEST_CLI defaults updated to ai/scripts/maintenance/

V-B-A composite lesson update (Subs 9/10/11/12):
Cross-cutting folder moves' substring-sweep file-type matrix needs
extension-less filenames (Dockerfile, Makefile) + shell scripts (.sh)
in addition to .mjs/.json/.yml/.yaml. Documentation files (.md) are
deliberately excluded — they describe historical context, not runtime
behavior. But operational artifacts (Dockerfiles, hooks, env scripts)
ARE runtime-load-bearing.

- 2026-05-23T20:37:18Z @tobiu referenced in commit `e365943` - "feat(agentos): extract DreamCoordinatorService — getDueTask only (#11858) (#11863)

* feat(agentos): extract DreamCoordinatorService — getDueTask only (#11858)

Authored by Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code). Session 5572d9a5-558d-4bea-b416-e31496c289c4.

FAIR-band: in-band [verify @ merge-gate]

Resolves #11858

Sub 15 of Epic #11831 — first of three coordinator extractions in Discussion
#11857 Cycle-3.5 B-prime convergence (graduated 2026-05-23T19:23Z, GPT
[GRADUATION_APPROVED] DC_kwDODSospM4BA-...). Matches D3.1 boundary per
v13-path.md:117: per-task coordinators decide what work is due; orchestrator
wires; ProcessSupervisor executes.

Creates `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/DreamCoordinatorService.mjs`:
- Pure-function `buildDreamTrigger({now, lastRunAt, intervalMs})` returns
  `{taskName, source, reason}` or null
- `DreamCoordinatorService.getDueTask({state, now, dreamIntervalMs})` wraps
  the pure function with state-shape mapping
- Mirrors `SummarizationCoordinatorService.getDueTask({...})` precedent shape

Strict boundary preserved:
- DreamService retains `processUndigestedSessions()` and all execution-side
  methods unchanged — coordinator owns due-trigger selection ONLY
- No state writes / no markStarted/markCompleted/recordTaskOutcome calls
- No reactive-config-aliased wrapper around DreamService (Sub-1 anti-pattern
  at new layer is explicitly avoided per Discussion #11857 Avoided Traps)
- Orchestrator's scheduleDreamCycle() body unchanged in this Sub; Sub 18
  #11862 wires the new coordinator into the registry

Pure-function unit tests: 8/8 pass covering interval-elapsed boundaries,
disabled (`intervalMs <= 0`), exactly-equal-to-interval edge, reason-carries-
interval-verbatim for telemetry, getDueTask state mapping (including
graceful missing-state default to lastRunAt=0).

* fix(agentos): switch DreamCoordinatorService to gatekeep pattern (#11858)

Same anti-pattern operator caught earlier today on Env (Sub 1 review):
class extending Base + singleton: true + Neo.setupClass for a service that
uses ZERO Neo features — no reactive configs, no lifecycle hooks, no
construct/apply/createId, no className/singleton semantics actually used.
Just a namespace for one pure-wrapper method around buildDreamTrigger.

Switched to gatekeep pattern per src/core/IdGenerator.mjs + src/util/Env.mjs
precedent: plain object literal + Neo.gatekeep(obj, 'Neo.x.y.Z'). Drops
Base inheritance overhead + setupClass processing + the empty static config.

Consumer surface unchanged (DreamCoordinatorService.getDueTask({...}) works
identically). 8/8 unit tests pass against the new shape.

Precedent debt acknowledged: SummarizationCoordinatorService /
BackupCoordinatorService / PrimaryRepoSyncService all have the same
anti-pattern (Base + singleton: true for stateless utilities). Worth a
follow-up sweep — flagged via spawn_task in this turn for separate cleanup.

* refactor(agentos): collapse DreamCoordinator's pure-fn/wrapper split — one getDueTask (#11858)

Operator caught the split as ceremony-without-payoff. Honest answer: I
mimicked SummarizationCoordinatorService's pattern (separate buildSummaryTrigger
pure function + getDueTask wrapper) without challenging whether the split
was earned. There it IS earned — SummarizationCoordinator.getDueTask calls
getUnreadSunsetHandoversFn(db) for additional I/O input + attaches an
onSuccess hook that marks handovers as read. The pure function takes 4
inputs across 2 branches; the wrapper does real extra work.

DreamCoordinator's getDueTask just maps state.lastRunAt ?? 0 into the
pure function's arg. No I/O, no side-effect hooks, no extra branches.
The split adds ceremony, not value.

Collapsed to single getDueTask({state, now, dreamIntervalMs}). Spec
collapses 8 → 5 tests (the redundant pure-vs-wrapper duplicates
disappear). Default export still uses Neo.gatekeep so the registry
descriptor shape (coordinator field with .getDueTask method) works
identically.

* refactor(agentos): DreamCoordinator pure module — drop gatekeep + default export (#11858)

Two issues operator caught on re-read:

1. Stale namespace path. Both the @namespace JSDoc tag and the Neo.gatekeep
   argument referenced `Neo.ai.daemons.services.DreamCoordinatorService`
   (the pre-Sub-8 path). Actual filesystem location is now
   `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/` → namespace would be
   `Neo.ai.daemons.orchestrator.services.DreamCoordinatorService`. Copied
   stale JSDoc from SummarizationCoord precedent without updating.

2. Double export of the same function. `getDueTask` was exported both as
   a named export AND wrapped in the gatekeep default-export object. One
   function exposed via two import paths = same ceremony-without-payoff
   anti-pattern from the prior commit, just at a different layer.

Deeper question: why register via Neo.gatekeep at all? Env / IdGenerator
use it because Neo.util.Env / Neo.core.IdGenerator are broadly-used
substrate where tooling/debug access via Neo.ns() has real value.
DreamCoordinator is orchestrator-internal — consumers import the function
directly; namespace registration buys nothing.

Final shape: pure module with one named export. No gatekeep. No default.
No @namespace JSDoc. Spec drops the Neo + core/_export bootstrap imports
too (not needed without module-load gatekeep call).

4/4 spec tests pass. Net diff vs prior commit: -36 LOC.

Sub 18's registry will consume via `import {getDueTask as dreamGetDueTask}
from '.../DreamCoordinatorService.mjs'` and register the function on a
descriptor — coordinator-as-object wrapper not needed.

* refactor(agentos): rename — not a Service, just a function in scheduling/ (#11858)

Operator: "this is not a service. it is a function." Service/Coordinator
suffixes imply state + methods + lifecycle; this file holds one pure
trigger projection. Files describe what they are.

Renames:
- ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/DreamCoordinatorService.mjs
  -> ai/daemons/orchestrator/scheduling/dream.mjs
- test/.../services/DreamCoordinatorService.spec.mjs
  -> test/.../scheduling/dream.spec.mjs

New folder scheduling/ (sibling to services/) holds per-task due-trigger
projections. services/ keeps things that are genuine services. Example:
SummarizationCoordinatorService calls getUnreadSunsetHandoversFn(db) and
attaches onSuccess hooks; that one EARNED the Service suffix. DreamService
/ SwarmHeartbeatService / GoldenPathSynthesizer stay in services/ as
execution-side responsibilities.

JSDoc + describe title updated. No more Coordinator/Service in this file
or its spec. 4/4 spec tests pass at new location.

Sub 18's registry will consume via:
  import {getDueTask as dreamGetDueTask} from './scheduling/dream.mjs'

Downstream churn flagged: Discussion #11857 body + tickets #11859/11860
still use ...CoordinatorService naming. Will update when filing Sub 16 /
Sub 17 work (same shape: scheduling/<task>.mjs). Sub 19 #11861 stays
Service-named since MaintenanceBackpressureService IS a genuine service
(state + multiple visibly-separate surfaces + lease delegation).

* docs(agentos): drop decay-prone JSDoc references in scheduling/dream.mjs (#11858)

Operator: "we e.g. change the referenced file, and then what? history versus
intents."

Removed brittle cross-references that lie when targets shift:
- v13-path.md:117 (line number — lies on any edit above line 117)
- D3.1 boundary (section label — lies on doc restructure)
- DreamService.processUndigestedSessions() (specific method name — lies on rename)
- @see ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/DreamService.mjs (scaffolding the
  reader can infer from file location + function name)

Same anti-pattern as Sub 1's historical-context comments: describes a
moment-in-time architecture state, not the file's own intent. JSDoc
should describe what THIS file IS so it stays true regardless of
neighboring substrate evolution.

Result: JSDoc now describes the function's contract (returns trigger
when interval elapsed since lastRunAt; pure). No external coordinates.
4/4 spec tests still pass.

Lesson for Sub 16/17/18/19: no baked line numbers, no section labels,
no neighboring-file method names in JSDoc prose.

* docs(agentos): collapse duplicate JSDoc blocks — one function, one comment (#11858)

Operator: file has ONE function, two comment blocks said the same thing
twice. File-level @summary made sense when this was a class with methods
(class JSDoc + method JSDoc). Now that it's a single pure function, the
file IS the function — one JSDoc above getDueTask covers both.

4/4 spec tests still pass.

- 2026-05-23T20:50:21Z @tobiu referenced in commit `ac2fecf` - "feat(agentos): extract swarm-heartbeat due-trigger projection (#11859) (#11865)

Sub 16 of Epic #11831. Pure function in scheduling/<task>.mjs, matching the
shape Sub 15 (PR #11863) established after 6 iterations of operator-led
KISS pressure. Applies all Sub-15 lessons from the start:

- File at scheduling/swarmHeartbeat.mjs (no Service/Coordinator suffix)
- One JSDoc block above the one function (no file-level + function-level split)
- No class extends Base, no singleton config, no Neo.gatekeep
- No decay-prone JSDoc references (no v13-path.md:N, no D3.1, no method names)
- Spec doesn't import Neo (no module-load gatekeep call requiring globalThis.Neo)
- Function signature parallel to scheduling/dream.mjs

4/4 spec tests pass.

Orchestrator's inline schedule logic (lines 906-934) is NOT modified yet —
Sub 18 #11862 wires the new projection into the registry. SwarmHeartbeatService
retains initAsync() + pulse() + all lifecycle/execution methods. The
enabled-gate (this.swarmHeartbeatEnabled) stays in Orchestrator's call-site
until Sub 18 moves profile-aware filtering into pickNextCandidate.

- 2026-05-25T05:26:05Z @tobiu referenced in commit `9836060` - "refactor(agentos): harmonize 3 Coordinator services to scheduling/<task>.mjs (#11864) (#11941)

* refactor(agentos): harmonize 3 Coordinator services to scheduling/<task>.mjs pure-function pattern (#11864)

Sub 20 of Epic #11831 — completes the scheduling/services boundary established by Sub 15 (PR #11863) for the 3 pre-existing coordinator services that retained class+singleton+Neo.setupClass machinery despite using zero Neo class-system features.

- Delete services/SummarizationCoordinatorService.mjs (superseded by scheduling/summary.mjs which already exists with the same shape)
- Delete services/BackupCoordinatorService.mjs (superseded by scheduling/backup.mjs)
- Slim services/PrimaryRepoSyncService.mjs: drop the getDueTask method + buildPrimaryRepoSyncTrigger export (both already in scheduling/primaryDevSync.mjs); keep runTask + execution helpers per the per-file split prescription in #11864
- Orchestrator.mjs: import {getDueTask as <name>Import} from ./scheduling/<task>.mjs for all 3; expose as function-typed instance-field DI seams (summaryGetDueTask, backupGetDueTask, primaryDevSyncGetDueTask) so tests still override without module-level mocking
- Update Orchestrator.spec.mjs: 4 mock injection sites swap from {getDueTask: fn} object-shape to function-shape; primaryRepoSyncService stays object-shape for runTask
- Update services/PrimaryRepoSyncService.spec.mjs: drop the 1 interval-triggers test (scheduling/primaryDevSync.spec.mjs has the canonical coverage)
- Update daemon.spec.mjs: tighten daemonSource maintenance-script assertions to quoted-filename form so scheduling/backup.mjs imports don't false-positive against the maintenance/backup.mjs guard
- daemon.mjs JSDoc: drop the SummarizationCoordinatorService reference from the bootstrap comment

Substrate-net-reduction: 2 source files + 2 spec files deleted; PrimaryRepoSyncService.mjs slimmed by 40 lines. Compression-positive per Discussion #11891.

* docs(agentos): update v13-path.md SummarizationCoordinatorService references to scheduling/summary.mjs (#11864)

Reconciles indexed architecture docs with the post-#11864 pure-function scheduling boundary. Future-agent KB rebuilds will no longer point at the removed `services/SummarizationCoordinatorService.mjs` precedent.

- Structure diagram (line 102) — now shows `scheduling/summary.mjs` as the Piece C trigger location with `services/PrimaryRepoSyncService.mjs` clarified as runTask-only post-#11864
- CadenceEngine boundary doc (line 117) — `getDueTask` precedent pointer updated to `scheduling/summary.mjs`; historical pointer to retired class preserved for archaeology
- M3 milestone narrative (line 169) — original SummarizationCoordinatorService text replaced with "summarization sweep coordinator" generic + parenthetical post-#11864 shape pointer
- M3 MVP split narrative (line 171) — "Piece C trigger selection" sentence rephrased so the active-pointer matches scheduling/summary.mjs
- M3.5 extraction-location precedent (line 188) — preserves historical SummarizationCoordinatorService.mjs line-233 reference but explicitly annotates that the precedent class was retired by #11864; the services/ landing-place decision for execution-shaped collaborators remains canonical

Addresses @neo-gpt cycle-1 RA on PR #11941: stale `learn/agentos/v13-path.md` references would otherwise borrow authority from the removed precedent in future KB rebuilds.

- 2026-05-25T05:49:31Z @tobiu referenced in commit `193b5f5` - "test(orchestrator): add Sub-1 invariant + config-precedence + propagation coverage (#11834) (#11945)

Sub 2 of Epic #11831 — locks the contract Sub 1 (#11833) established by adding the regression-detection layer that prevents the removed anti-patterns from re-entering.

New spec file Orchestrator.invariants.spec.mjs (16/16 pass) covering 3 AC clusters:

**AC2 — Config-precedence (6 tests)**:
- Env var overrides AiConfig.data for interval values
- AiConfig.data is consulted when env var is absent
- Env var overrides AiConfig.data for boolean enable flags (localOnly)
- AiConfig.localOnly.X=null falls through to deployment-profile default (local enables, cloud disables)
- AiConfig.data mutations are isolated per test via beforeEach/afterEach restore (substrate-leak prevention)

**AC3 — Parent-prop propagation via afterSetX hooks (5 tests, witness-property style)**:
- taskDefinitions → processSupervisorService
- dataDir → processSupervisorService + maintenanceBackpressureService
- healthService → both (witness via marker property)
- taskStateService → both (witness via marker)
- spawnFn → processSupervisorService (witness via marker on function)
- heavyMaintenanceLeasePath → maintenanceBackpressureService

Propagation uses witness-property assertions rather than reference equality because child reactive setters may wrap inputs via ClassSystemUtil.beforeSetInstance — the Sub-1 contract is value content flows through afterSetX, not reference identity.

**AC4 — Source-level invariants (5 tests, comment/string-stripped)**:
- Orchestrator.mjs has no `configure()` shadow-resolver method
- TaskDefinitions.mjs has no `DEFAULT_*_INTERVAL_MS` exports
- Orchestrator.mjs has no `parseInterval` / `parseEnabledFlag` call sites
- Orchestrator.mjs has no `processSupervisorService.set({...this...})` context-replay block
- Orchestrator.mjs has no `_`-suffix reactive config slot without a corresponding `beforeSet*` or `afterSet*` hook

Inline stripCommentsAndStrings helper handles block comments, line comments, and string literals so source-grep doesn't false-positive against JSDoc that explicitly names what the class doesn't do (lines 124-125 of Orchestrator.mjs document the absences).

cloudOnly handling is defensive — save+restore only if present on origin/dev. The cloudOnly collection lands on dev when #11940 merges; until then, this spec runs cleanly without depending on it.

AC1 (refactored createTestOrchestrator) is the helper Sub 1 (#11833) already shipped; Sub 2 layers the invariant-test coverage on top. Total touched-surface: 232/232 orchestrator-tree unit tests pass.

- 2026-05-25T05:50:23Z @tobiu referenced in commit `5d428ab` - "test(orchestrator): external-config audit invariants AC1-3 + AC4-5 findings (#11837) (#11946)

* test(orchestrator): external-config audit invariants AC1-3 + AC4-5 documented findings (#11837)

Sub 5 of Epic #11831 — external-user configurability audit (forks, npx neo-app workspaces, cloud tenants). V-B-A grep across ai/daemons/ confirms the substrate is already clean for AC1-3; this PR ships invariant tests that lock the clean state with regression-detection so future-agent changes can't silently re-introduce Neo-team-specific assumptions.

New spec file Orchestrator.externalConfig.spec.mjs (3/3 pass) covering:

**AC1 — No Neo-team identity literals (@neo-opus-4-7 / @neo-gpt / @neo-gemini-3-1-pro)** across the ai/daemons/ subtree (includes the orchestrator-daemon entrypoint at ai/daemons/orchestrator/daemon.mjs post-#11847 thematic split). Daemon code must resolve identities via the ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs registry, never via hardcoded literals.

**AC2 — No hardcoded "Project 12" / "v13" / "release:v*" literals** in daemon code. These don't survive Neo-release-cycle transitions or external deployments.

**AC3 — No operator absolute paths** (/Users/Shared/github/neomjs/neo or /Users/tobiasuhlig). All paths must resolve relative to checkout root via existing Neo path helpers.

The walker recurses ai/daemons/ skipping node_modules, dist, .git, .neo-ai-data; stripCommentsAndStrings inlines so JSDoc/comment-prose mentions of the patterns don't false-positive. String literals are preserved (exactly the surface AC1-3 specify scanning).

**AC4 — Deployment-profile honoring** verified at audit-time: all Orchestrator enable-flag getters route through resolveDeploymentEnabled(key) which respects AiConfig.orchestrator.deploymentMode (Orchestrator.mjs:347-351). localOnly substrate populated symmetrically per daemon.mjs:229-261. Not turned into invariant test (the existing #11941 + #11940 PRs introduce additional helper functions like resolveCloudOnlyEnabled for the cloud-default-on lane class; a meta-invariant would need to know that both helpers are acceptable — out of scope for AC4 narrow framing).

**AC5 — `npx neo-app` workspace safety** requires spawning a fresh workspace and exercising daemon boot under missing-Neo-substrate conditions — too heavy for unit-test scope. Deferred to follow-up (will file ticket if not already tracked).

Test-spec-only PR. 3/3 PASS at branch head; ai/daemons substrate unchanged. Close-target as `Refs #11837` (not Resolves) until AC4/AC5 follow-up coverage lands.

* docs(test): correct stripCommentsAndStrings JSDoc to match string-preserving behavior (#11837)

Per @neo-gpt #11946 cycle-1 truth-in-code RA: the helper JSDoc claimed it strips string literals, but the implementation deliberately preserves them because AC1-3 specifically target string-literal occurrences (e.g., '@neo-opus-4-7' as a literal is a violation, but a comment mentioning the same handle is not). Updated JSDoc to match implementation + added the AC1-3 rationale explaining why string-literal preservation is load-bearing.
